title: Guess Whos Coming To Criticize Dinner

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with typical Homer mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation after yet another accident
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment, perfectly economical, but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: I hope you all enjoy your ride to... and tour of the Springfield Shopper newspaper. Groundskeeper Willie and I will stay behind... to remove all traces of asbestos and the word 'evolution' from our school.
jokes.setup: Standard field trip announcement
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of censoring evolution alongside dangerous asbestos removal
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire equating scientific fact with physical danger, very Simpsons-esque social commentary

jokes.text: Next stop, Margaritaville! Uh- Oh, they're still here. Yes.
jokes.setup: Skinner's inappropriate excitement about the kids leaving
jokes.punchline: Awkward realization that the children can hear his relief
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's inappropriate relief, well-structured awkwardness

jokes.text: Come to think of it, I haven't seen Uter since the last field trip. [Chuckling] Uter? I don't remember any Uter. [Chuckling] Silly name. Uter.
jokes.setup: Innocent comment about missing student
jokes.punchline: Immediate cover-up suggesting something sinister happened to Uter
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor suggesting the faculty murdered a student, with Willie's nervous denial making it funnier

jokes.text: Don't worry, sweetie. Daddy's got it covered. [Homer singing] I work hard for the money... Turn tape over!
jokes.setup: Homer claims he has work covered while volunteering to drive
jokes.punchline: Revealed he's just playing a tape of himself singing to fool his boss
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer laziness scheme, perfectly in character with good visual payoff

jokes.text: Now, there's an employee, Smithers. A smile on his lips and a song in his heart. Promote him.
jokes.setup: Burns hearing Homer's obvious work-avoidance scheme
jokes.punchline: Burns completely misreads the situation and wants to promote Homer
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Burns being completely oblivious to reality

jokes.text: I spy with my little eye... something beginning with 'D.' Dingus! God bless you, Nelson Muntz. I'm no hero. I just like to hit people in the head.
jokes.setup: Innocent I Spy game on the bus
jokes.punchline: Nelson guesses 'Dingus' and reveals his simple motivation for violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson characterization with his honest simplicity about violence being unexpectedly endearing

jokes.text: Hey, you [Beep]! You cut me off! Oh, yeah! [Beep] you! Dad! That's an ambulance! Oh, right. [Beep] ambulance! Think you're so big with your [Beep Beep] siren... and your letters on backwards!
jokes.setup: Homer road raging at another driver
jokes.punchline: He continues cursing at an ambulance even after being told what it is, then complains about backwards letters
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Homer's complete inability to back down even when obviously wrong

jokes.text: Think you're so big with your [Beep Beep] siren and your letters on backwards!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to rage at the ambulance
jokes.punchline: Complains about backward 'AMBULANCE' lettering as if it's pretentious
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Homer sees mirror lettering as arrogance rather than necessity

jokes.text: Here we are, kids. The zoo. Well, that's great, Dad. Except you were supposed to drive us to the newspaper.
jokes.setup: Homer announcing their arrival
jokes.punchline: He drove to the wrong destination entirely
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer incompetence, predictable but functional

jokes.text: If he's so smart, how come he's dead?
jokes.setup: Tour guide explaining newspaper founder's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Bart's illogical but confident dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - completely wrong but delivered with total confidence. Economy is flawless.

jokes.text: Over the years, the Shopper merged... with the Springfield Times, Post, Globe, Herald, Jewish News and Hot Sex Weekly... to become Springfield's number-one newspaper.
jokes.setup: Standard newspaper merger explanation
jokes.punchline: 'Hot Sex Weekly' casually included in the list
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with the inappropriate publication slipped into corporate speak

jokes.text: Hi! Are you interested in a subscription to the Shopper? Low introductory rates! No, you gotta help ol' Gil. What's it gonna take to keep you on the phone? Dance for ya? But you wouldn't even see it. You- [Chuckling] All right. I'm dancin'!
jokes.setup: Gil doing telemarketing at the newspaper
jokes.punchline: Desperately offering to dance over the phone, then actually doing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gil characterization - pathetically desperate and illogical but endearing

jokes.text: Who here reads Mary Worth? Let's move on.
jokes.setup: Tour guide asking about popular comic strip
jokes.punchline: Immediate awkward silence prompts quick topic change
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective acknowledgment of Mary Worth's lack of popularity

jokes.text: This is where we store Ann Landers and Dear Abby for their 23 hours of daily sleep. My advice is to free us or let us die!
jokes.setup: Explanation of advice columnist storage
jokes.punchline: They're apparently imprisoned and pleading for freedom or death
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and surreal take on syndicated columnists, treating them like livestock

jokes.text: Ooh! Pointless nostalgia!
jokes.setup: Machine can print headlines from your birth date
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic response labels it accurately
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of accurate self-awareness, delivered with characteristic enthusiasm

jokes.text: I'd sure hate to be him. [Laughs, Groans]
jokes.setup: Homer reading about someone's misfortune from his birth date
jokes.punchline: Not realizing he's reading about himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness, standard but effective irony

jokes.text: And to protect Mother Earth... each copy contains a certain percentage of recycled paper. What percent is that? Zero! Zero's a percent.
jokes.setup: Environmental claim about recycled paper
jokes.punchline: Zero percent recycled content, with Homer's illogical defense
jokes.explanation: Good environmental satire with Homer's mathematically incorrect but confident defense

jokes.text: Hey, I smell cake. Cake that says- [Sniffs] 'Farewell' and- [Sniffs, Gasps] 'Best Wishes!' Your old man has an awesome nose. Oh, that's nothing. He can hear pudding.
jokes.setup: Homer demonstrating his powerful sense of smell
jokes.punchline: Lisa's escalation that he can hear pudding
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from impressive to impossible, 'hearing pudding' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: What can I say except thanks for the predictable champagne... pizza that's hardly numero uno... and ice cream cake which reminds us... why make 31 flavors when you can't get vanilla right?
jokes.setup: Retirement party for food critic
jokes.punchline: She can't help but give scathing reviews even at her own farewell party
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - she's so ingrained as a critic she can't turn it off

jokes.text: pizza that's hardly numero uno and ice cream cake which reminds us why make 31 flavors when you can't get vanilla right?
jokes.setup: Food critic's farewell speech
jokes.punchline: Harsh critique of retirement party food
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for the food critic - can't help being critical even at own party

jokes.text: I wouldn't want to be married to her. I mean, again.
jokes.setup: Editor commenting on the harsh critic
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that he was married to her
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy, reveals personal history

jokes.text: Oh, I like food, all right. I like pizza, I like bagels, I like hot dogs with mustard and beer... I'll eat eggplant, I could even eat a baby deer... Who's that baby deer on the lawn there? Enough already!
jokes.setup: Homer enthusiastically describing his love of food in song
jokes.punchline: Escalates to eating baby deer and pointing one out, editor cuts him off
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's indiscriminate appetite, musical format adds charm

jokes.text: No, it usually takes a few hours.
jokes.setup: Editor looking for someone who doesn't immediately dismiss food
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation about his digestion timeline
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misunderstanding with excellent economy and deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Thanks for the chance. You won't never regret this, Mr. Editing Guy.
jokes.setup: Homer accepting the job offer
jokes.punchline: Double negative and calling him 'Mr. Editing Guy'
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer malapropism, functional but not special

jokes.text: We don't need no stinkin' 'E.'
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out broken typewriter
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissal referencing 'Treasure of Sierra Madre'
jokes.explanation: Movie reference shows some wit but fairly predictable

jokes.text: 'Food Box- Go or No Go, by Homer--' Earl- No! Bill Simpson!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with review titles and his byline
jokes.punchline: Randomly changing his name to Earl, then Bill Simpson
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's scattered thinking process, mildly amusing name confusion

jokes.text: Well, you keep using words like 'pasghetti' and 'momatoes.' You make numerous threatening references to the U.N. And at the end you repeat the words 'Screw Flanders'... over and over again.
jokes.setup: Editor explaining what's wrong with Homer's review
jokes.punchline: Escalating list from mispronunciations to UN threats to obsessive Flanders hatred
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Homer's complete inability to focus, 'pasghetti' and 'momatoes' are perfectly wrong

jokes.text: You make numerous threatening references to the U.N. And at the end you repeat the words 'Screw Flanders' over and over again.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of problems with Homer's review
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd content including anti-UN sentiment and Flanders obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Homer's inability to focus, with characteristic Flanders hatred

jokes.text: Oh, it's so hard to get to 500 words.
jokes.setup: Editor rejecting Homer's terrible review
jokes.punchline: Homer's only concern is word count, not quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - focusing on quantity over quality, very relatable

jokes.text: Still not clean. Stink of failure still on me.
jokes.setup: Homer washing himself after embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Believes failure has a literal smell that won't wash off
jokes.explanation: Nice metaphor made literal, shows Homer's shame in physical terms

jokes.text: Well, we went to Pâté LaBelle last week. How about that? Great. Now, let me think. The food was- Mmm- not undelicious. The food is delicious. [Gasps] That's brilliant.
jokes.setup: Lisa helping Homer write a proper review
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'not undelicious' transformed to simple 'delicious' amazes him
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic showing Lisa's intelligence versus Homer's simple amazement at basic language

jokes.text: Well, we went to Pâté LaBelle last week. How about that?
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting a restaurant to review
jokes.punchline: Pun on Patti LaBelle's name for fancy restaurant
jokes.explanation: Decent pun showing Lisa's intelligence, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Hmm. What's the English equivalent for [Groaning]? I'd say 'transcendent.' How about 'groin-grabbingly transcendent'?
jokes.setup: Homer making satisfied eating sounds, Lisa translating to sophisticated language
jokes.punchline: Homer's vulgar modification of the elegant word 'transcendent'
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between Lisa's sophistication and Homer's crudeness, 'groin-grabbingly' became a memorable Simpsons phrase

jokes.text: 497, 498 words. How about 'Screw Flanders'? Bon appétit. Ehh, both's good.
jokes.setup: Lisa counting words to reach 500
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting his Flanders obsession again, then agreeing any ending works
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Flanders obsession, shows Homer's consistent fixations

jokes.text: Not bad! Not bad at all! We're gonna run this on page one... of section H-2. Whoo-hoo! Stop the presses! [Gasps] [Alarms Blaring] Okay, start the presses! That takes four hours. Whatever. I'll be at Moe's.
jokes.setup: Editor praising review for page one placement, Homer excited to stop presses
jokes.punchline: Page one of obscure section H-2, stopping presses takes 4 hours, Homer loses interest
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's excitement with bureaucratic realities and his short attention span

jokes.text: Marge. Shh. It's important that no one knows that I'm a food critic. Hear that, Maude? Homer's a critic. Homer's a critic. Pass it on.
jokes.setup: Homer insisting his identity be kept secret
jokes.punchline: Immediately shouting his identity to neighbor and telling her to spread it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - says one thing, immediately does the opposite

jokes.text: Did you hear? Homer's a critic. Quit changing the subject! Where is Uter? We just want closure.
jokes.setup: Gossip about Homer spreading
jokes.punchline: Someone trying to redirect attention back to missing Uter
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Uter mystery, shows how gossip distracts from real issues

jokes.text: Quit changing the subject! Where is Uter? We just want closure.
jokes.setup: Gossip about Homer's job
jokes.punchline: Parents still obsessing over missing student
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark callback to earlier Uter joke, shows persistent parental concern

jokes.text: Arr! Here you are. One critic's special. If anything appears to be movin'... that's just freshness.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain serving special meal for critic
jokes.punchline: Warning that moving food is just 'freshness' (implying it's alive/spoiled)
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting Sea Captain moment with great euphemistic language

jokes.text: If you could just get somebody to pay you for scratching your butt, we'll be on easy street.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating getting paid to eat
jokes.punchline: Marge identifying his other major activity that could be monetized
jokes.explanation: Good marital comedy with Marge's practical assessment of Homer's 'skills'

jokes.text: Wah! My first published article! Although someone else's name is on it. Heh-heh. Welcome to the humiliating world of professional writing.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about being published
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical but accurate assessment of ghostwriting
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on writing industry, Homer accidentally wise about exploitation

jokes.text: Welcome to Planet Springfield... the restaurant owned by me, Chuck Norris... Johnny Carson's third wife and the Russian mafia.
jokes.setup: Standard themed restaurant introduction
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre ownership including Russian mafia
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and satire of themed restaurants with random celebrity connections

jokes.text: [Gasps] There's the coffee mug from Heartbeeps! And there's the cane from Citizen Kane! Wait a minute. There was no cane in Citizen Kane.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about movie memorabilia
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out fake memorabilia from a famous film
jokes.explanation: Smart joke about fake Hollywood memorabilia, requires film knowledge to fully appreciate

jokes.text: And there's that awful script from The Cable Guy. Lemme see. Stupid script! Nearly wrecked Jim Carrey's career!
jokes.setup: More movie memorabilia examination
jokes.punchline: Homer's harsh but accurate criticism of The Cable Guy
jokes.explanation: Topical movie criticism, decent but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Hey, Homer! Great call on that chicken place. And on that rib place! I never knew everything was so good!
jokes.setup: Carl praising Homer's restaurant recommendations
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Homer gives everything positive reviews
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's lack of critical standards, functional setup for later plot

jokes.text: Yes, Simpson, your love of food is contagious. I've never felt jollier. [Cracking] Ooh. There go my shin bones again.
jokes.setup: Burns expressing unusual happiness
jokes.punchline: His bones breaking from the unfamiliar emotion of joy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns characterization - so physically frail that positive emotions break his bones

jokes.text: Are you gonna fire me for swiping office supplies? No. Whew! [Clacking]
jokes.setup: Homer worried about being fired
jokes.punchline: Relief about theft, followed by sound of continued stealing
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his immediate return to bad behavior when consequences are removed

jokes.text: Why, you- You made them cancel Platypus Man! And The Cosby Mysteries! That show had limitless possibilities!
jokes.setup: Homer meeting TV critic
jokes.punchline: Blaming critic for canceling obscure, terrible shows, praising Cosby Mysteries
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how audiences blame critics for bad shows being cancelled

jokes.text: Just got back from the gopher poison show in Paris. Let me tell you something- the days of clubbing them with a baseball bat are over! For you, perhaps.
jokes.setup: Mundane farm supply criticism
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual endorsement of violent gopher killing
jokes.explanation: Absurd specialty critic met with Homer's casual violence, good contrast

jokes.text: Everything's a rave. 'Nine thumbs up'? What the hell is that?
jokes.setup: Critics complaining about Homer's reviews
jokes.punchline: Impossible rating system of nine thumbs
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on review rating inflation, economical and quotable

jokes.text: The only bad review you gave was to a slice of pizza you found under the couch. It lost some points 'cause it had a Hot Wheel on it.
jokes.setup: Pointing out Homer's lack of bad reviews
jokes.punchline: His one bad review was for pizza with a toy car on it
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific detail that perfectly captures Homer's low standards and domestic chaos

jokes.text: We see 'John Deere has come out with this year's line of rototillers. Surprise, surprise- they're green! I say it's time to send John Deere a 'Dear John.''
jokes.setup: Professional farm equipment criticism
jokes.punchline: 'Dear John' pun on John Deere company name
jokes.explanation: Groan-worthy pun that's intentionally bad, shows the mundane nature of specialty criticism

jokes.text: Lord, thy daughters... Goneril, Regan and Cordelia. What is this, merry old England or Petticoat Junction?
jokes.setup: Homer watching/reviewing King Lear
jokes.punchline: Comparing Shakespeare to 1960s sitcom
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash joke showing Homer's lowbrow perspective on high art

jokes.text: This pea soup is as weak as the acting and nowhere near as hammy.
jokes.setup: Homer reviewing both play and restaurant food
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'hammy' acting versus actual ham in soup
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning pun that works on multiple levels, economical construction

jokes.text: The other critics told me to be mean. You should always give in to peer pressure. But what if someone bad tells me to- Always!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his mean reviews, Lisa questioning peer pressure advice
jokes.punchline: Homer's absolute commitment to always following peer pressure
jokes.explanation: Good parenting satire with Homer giving terrible life advice with complete confidence

jokes.text: Oh, I got one! How do you make a King Lear? Put the queen in a bikini! Here's another one. 'Knock knock.' 'Who's there?' 'Juliet.' 'Juliet who?' Juliet so much pasta fazool, Romeo doesn't want her anymore!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to improve Shakespeare with comedy
jokes.punchline: Terrible puns mixing Shakespeare with bad stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad jokes that show Homer's poor comedy instincts, too long and hacky

jokes.text: Juliet so much pasta fazool, Romeo doesn't want her anymore!
jokes.setup: Homer's knock-knock joke setup
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on 'you let' and ethnic stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Deliberately awful pun with ethnic stereotyping, showing Homer's terrible comedy instincts

jokes.text: Whoa, tough crowd. They're booing Shakespeare!
jokes.setup: Audience booing Homer's additions to the play
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming Shakespeare instead of his own terrible jokes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion, refusing to take responsibility for audience reaction

jokes.text: Uh, not bad... if lasagna is Italian for 'pile of puke'! Aah! I chopp-a you good! Well, I hope you cut me better than you did these string beans. Hmm. I seem to be missing a piece of my ear. Touché.
jokes.setup: Homer giving brutal restaurant review
jokes.punchline: Chef attacks him, Homer critiques the attack, then admires the chef's skill after losing ear
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Homer maintaining critic perspective even while being mutilated

jokes.text: Sorry, Marge. I'm afraid this gets my lowest rating ever- seven thumbs up.
jokes.setup: Homer critiquing Marge's cooking apologetically
jokes.punchline: His 'lowest rating ever' is still seven thumbs up
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Homer's inflation problem, with the irony that his harsh criticism is still overwhelmingly positive

jokes.text: Marge, I'm sorry, but your cooking's only got two moves- shake and bake. You like Shake 'n Bake! You used to put it in your coffee!
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing Marge's cooking style
jokes.punchline: Marge revealing Homer's own disgusting eating habits
jokes.explanation: Good marital comedy with Marge effectively countering Homer's criticism with his own grossness

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? What's a palate? Oh, it's a... special time in a boy's life when- Gotta go!
jokes.setup: Marge challenging Homer's sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing 'palate' with puberty explanation, then fleeing
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion moment with effective embarrassed exit, shows his pretension is hollow

jokes.text: So come to The Legless Frog... if you want to get sick and die and leave a big, garlicky corpse! P.S. Parking was ample.
jokes.setup: Homer giving devastating restaurant review
jokes.punchline: Adding positive note about parking after describing death
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between devastating criticism and mundane positive detail, very quotable

jokes.text: People will think what I tell them to think when you tell me what to tell them to think.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what people will think
jokes.punchline: Homer's convoluted logic about influence and collaboration
jokes.explanation: Confusing wordplay that shows Homer's garbled thinking, but too convoluted to be effective

jokes.text: Nobody talks to me that way! I'm Homer Simpson, the most powerful food critic in town... who will never get his comeuppance! You hear me? No comeuppance!
jokes.setup: Homer declaring his power and immunity from consequences
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declaring he'll never face comeuppance (which he obviously will)
jokes.explanation: Classic hubris moment that sets up the inevitable downfall, good dramatic irony

jokes.text: The food at the Gilded Truffle really, uh-What's a good word? [Sucking] Sucks! That's great! And the bread was really- Come on! Help me out here! Ruff!. Rough? I don't know. You've been pitchin' that all night.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to write negative reviews without Lisa
jokes.punchline: Getting writing help from the dog, rejecting dog's consistent suggestion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment treating the dog as a writing collaborator, with Homer being picky about the dog's suggestions

jokes.text: Ruff! Rough? I don't know. You've been pitchin' that all night. Chewy? Chewy! That's inspired!
jokes.setup: Homer getting writing help from the dog
jokes.punchline: Treats dog's barks as creative suggestions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - Homer collaborating with dog on writing

jokes.text: Homer, what gives with this review? You say 'the salad tastes like bark'... and the potatoes were very 'Grrrrr!' This reads like it was written by a dog. Are you crazy? A dog can't type. [Mutters] Unfortunately.
jokes.setup: Editor noting dog-like quality of review
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal denial that dogs can type, then muttering regret
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that confirms the dog writing theory, Homer's 'Unfortunately' is brilliantly absurd

jokes.text: This reads like it was written by a dog. Are you crazy? A dog can't type. [Mutters] Unfortunately.
jokes.setup: Editor criticizing Homer's poor writing
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal response and muttered disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback - editor unknowingly hits the truth, Homer deflects with logic

jokes.text: Astrology, Broom-Hilda, vacation horror stories... articles about Chronic Fatigue Syndrome- you know, chick crap.
jokes.setup: Editor explaining lifestyle section expectations
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing women's interests as 'chick crap'
jokes.explanation: Dated sexist humor that shows newspaper editor's casual misogyny, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Homer, he's outta control-a. He gave me a bad-a review! So my friend put a horse head on his bed. He ate-a the head and gave it a bad review! True story.
jokes.setup: Luigi complaining about Homer's review
jokes.punchline: Godfather reference backfires as Homer eats the horse head and reviews it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Godfather horse head threat - only Homer would eat it and review it negatively. Excellent escalation.

jokes.text: Arr, it covered up the 'D' from the health inspector.
jokes.setup: Question about why he displayed bad review
jokes.punchline: Using it to hide poor health grade
jokes.explanation: Good practical joke about restaurants hiding health violations, economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Are we restauranteurs, or are we murderers? Does that answer your question?
jokes.setup: Akira asking if they should kill Homer
jokes.punchline: Luigi's gesture apparently indicating they're murderers
jokes.explanation: Good setup for restaurant murder plot, relies on visual gag we can't see clearly

jokes.text: This will be Homer Simpson's last lagniappe.
jokes.setup: French chef planning Homer's death
jokes.punchline: Using sophisticated culinary term 'lagniappe' for last meal pun
jokes.explanation: Clever use of culinary vocabulary for murder pun, shows chef's sophistication even in villainy

jokes.text: Then he'll get his just dessert.
jokes.setup: Planning to kill Homer with food
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'just deserts' and literal dessert
jokes.explanation: Standard but effective pun on common phrase, fits the food murder plot

jokes.text: Come on! You're gonna kill him with a pastry? I've seen this man eat a bowl of change.
jokes.setup: Questioning effectiveness of pastry murder weapon
jokes.punchline: Reference to Homer eating coins
jokes.explanation: 'Bowl of change' is a perfect absurd detail that captures Homer's indiscriminate eating habits, very quotable

jokes.text: This éclair has over one million calories. Twenty-five pounds of butter per square inch. Covered with chocolate so dark... light cannot escape its surface.
jokes.setup: Describing the deadly éclair
jokes.punchline: Escalating from calories to physics-defying chocolate darkness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from nutritional excess to physics metaphor, 'light cannot escape' is wonderfully overwrought

jokes.text: Death by chocolate. [Laughs] Oh. And poison. I'll stick in some poison.
jokes.setup: Chef proud of chocolate death concept
jokes.punchline: Casually adding actual poison as backup
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of elaborate murder scheme with simple poison addition, shows chef's practical side

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, something bad usually happens to me when I go in anywhere. [Whistling] D'oh! Ohh! [Grunting] A bat. Now, that's a new one.
jokes.setup: Marge warning Homer about danger, Homer dismissing it
jokes.punchline: Immediate accident involving a bat, Homer's casual reaction
jokes.explanation: Good immediate payoff of Marge's warning with typical Homer accident, casual reaction to unusual mishap

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, something bad usually happens to me when I go in anywhere.
jokes.setup: Marge warning about intuition
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact acceptance of his bad luck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness about his disaster-prone nature

jokes.text: Lard ho! Arr, 'tis a good sign. Homer's unfastened the top button on his pants. Uh, no, he's been walking around like that since Thanksgiving.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain noting Homer's pants as sign of fullness
jokes.punchline: Luigi revealing it's just Homer's normal state since November
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization showing his permanent state of post-meal expansion

jokes.text: I'm surprised he just doesn't give it up and go for sweat pants. He says the crotch wears out too fast. Yarrr! That's gonna replace the whale in my nightmares!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer's clothing choices
jokes.punchline: TMI about crotch wear, Sea Captain's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with good reaction beat from Sea Captain, whale nightmare reference is nice character touch

jokes.text: Yarrr! That's gonna replace the whale in my nightmares!
jokes.setup: Learning about Homer's clothing problems
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain's horror replaces his usual whale trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sea Captain character moment - maritime trauma replaced by worse horror

jokes.text: [Sighs] Only your father could take a part-time job at a small-town paper... and wind up the target of international assassins.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining the danger Homer is in
jokes.punchline: Marge's weary summary of how Homer escalates simple situations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge observation that captures Homer's talent for escalating mundane situations into chaos

jokes.text: Ooh, that looks scrumdiddly-doodly-duddley- Get lost! A rude Frenchman? Well, I never!
jokes.setup: Ned approaching with characteristic enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Chef's rudeness shocking Ned's expectations of French politeness
jokes.explanation: Good Ned characterization with his innocent shock at rudeness, plays on French stereotypes

jokes.text: Dad, no! It's gonna kill you! Ehh, I've had a good run. [Grunting] Don't, uh- Um, it's low-fat! No!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to stop Homer from eating the deadly éclair
jokes.punchline: Homer's resignation to death, Lisa's desperate lie about it being low-fat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Homer's acceptance of fate and Lisa's smart appeal to his health concerns

jokes.text: Take him into custardy, boys! Hah! Lou and his frittatas! [Laughing] Frittatas! Oh, he likes eggs.
jokes.setup: Police arriving to arrest the chef
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's 'custardy' pun and ongoing Lou frittata joke
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum wordplay with callback to earlier frittata discussion, shows police camaraderie

jokes.text: Whew! That was close. Thank God it landed in that smoking crater!
jokes.setup: Éclair thrown away and lands somewhere
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks smoking crater is a good landing spot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - sees dangerous crater as fortunate disposal

jokes.text: Lisa, the important thing is I didn't get my comeuppance, and I never will
jokes.setup: Homer thanking Lisa for saving him
jokes.punchline: Immediately restating his immunity to comeuppance right before getting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for immediate ironic payoff, callback to earlier hubris moment

jokes.text: Run! [All Shouting] [Elephants Trumpeting] [Homer Groaning, Whimpering] [Homer] I'm finally getting my comeuppanceI
jokes.setup: Zoo animals charging at Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer finally acknowledging his comeuppance while being trampled
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the comeuppance setup, using zoo animals from opening scene ties everything together

tmdb_overview: With Lisa's help, Homer uses his vast knowledge of food to become the town's leading restaurant critic. But when he gets petty and vindictive in his criticisms, all the other chefs in town try to kill him.
